2010-07-01.title: Turkish Aid From Flotilla Begins Arriving in Gaza
2010-07-01.abstract: The aid arrives one month after Israeli commandos killed nine Turks in a raid on the flotilla.

2010-07-01.title: Setting an Example of Austerity at the Élysée Palace
2010-07-01.abstract: France is pushing its ministers to reduce their lavish expenses and bloated staffs after embarrassing disclosures of their misbehavior.

2010-07-01.title: Economies in Latin America Race Ahead
2010-07-01.abstract: With recovery fragile elsewhere, Latin America benefits from strong commodity demand and past economic measures.

2010-07-01.title: Top French Schools, Asked to Diversify, Fear for Standards
2010-07-01.abstract: The ideal of  race-blind meritocracy is being tested by the efforts at the elite universities that produce the nation’s leaders.

2010-07-01.title: Killers Stalk Politicians as Iraq Seeks Government
2010-07-01.abstract: Some 150 political figures have been assassinated since the election in March created a power vacuum.

2010-07-02.title: Decades of Untapped Authority
2010-07-02.abstract: Before he became pope, Benedict XVI held a critical decision-making position in the Vatican that had authority over cases involving priests who sexually molested children, but did not assert it for many years.

2010-07-02.title: Church Office Failed to Act on Abuse Scandal
2010-07-02.abstract: Before he became pope, Benedict XVI held a position that had authority over abuse cases but did not assert it for many years.

2010-07-02.title: China Puts Best Face Forward With News Channel
2010-07-02.abstract: The government’s official news agency is introducing an English-language, 24-hour news channel that will have a newsroom in Times Square.

2010-07-02.title: A Math Problem Solver Declines a $1 Million Prize
2010-07-02.abstract: This is not the first time that Grigory Perelman, a reclusive Russian mathematician, has turned down an award for unraveling one of the world’s most intractable math problems.

2010-07-02.title: Two Competing Visions of a European Economy
2010-07-02.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany is demanding fiscal rigor, while President Nicolas Sarkozy of France favors more room for stimulus spending.

2010-07-03.title: A Voice in Egypt for an Arab Age of Reason
2010-07-03.abstract: Ismail Serageldin, who directs the successor to the library of Alexandria, uses his position to counter what he calls “pseudoreligious fanaticism.”

2010-07-03.title: Despite Raid, Mostly Business as Usual for Israel and Turkey
2010-07-03.abstract: The investment and trade that continue beneath the surface are reminders of the deep and interconnected ties that Turkey and Israel have forged over the years.

2010-07-03.title: Mentor Says McChrystal Is ‘Crushed’ by the Change in His Circumstances
2010-07-03.abstract: Forced out, but allowed to retire with four stars, Gen. Stanley A. McChrystal is enduring a stunning reversal of fortunes.

2010-07-03.title: A Mission in the Desert
2010-07-03.abstract: A mission in the insurgent badlands of northern Iraq provided a vivid example of what American forces still do on the ground and, military officers said, would be able to do after the August deadline for the end of the combat mission in Iraq.

2010-07-03.title: War in Iraq Defies U.S. Timetable for End of Combat
2010-07-03.abstract: Even after a reduction in troop numbers, American forces will still hunt terrorists after the August deadline that President Obama set for the end of the combat mission in Iraq.

2010-07-04.title: Jailed by the K.G.B.’s Successor
2010-07-04.abstract: The prosecution of a mayor in Siberia shows how the F.S.B. operates in today’s Russia with virtually unchecked power.

2010-07-04.title: Petraeus Takes Command of Afghan Mission
2010-07-04.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus took command of the troubled Afghan war in a ceremony, warning of hard days to come but promising to persevere.

2010-07-04.title: Acting President in Poland Wins a Narrow Victory
2010-07-04.abstract: Poland’s acting president fended off a stiff electoral challenge on Sunday from the twin brother of the leader who died in a plane crash in April.

2010-07-04.title: Government in Pakistan Calls Meeting on Terrorism
2010-07-04.abstract: In an unusual sign of accord, Prime Minister Yusuf Raza Gilani said that the government and the opposition would hold a conference on ways to combat terrorism.

2010-07-04.title: French Scandals Over Expenses Force 2 to Resign
2010-07-04.abstract: Two French government ministers resigned suddenly on Sunday, apparently pushed by President Nicolas Sarkozy, after new attention to spending scandals.

2010-07-05.title: Sumo Figures Barred in Japan for Gambling
2010-07-05.abstract: Sumo’s governing body issued a lifetime ban on Keiji Tamiya for betting tens of thousands of dollars on professional baseball games, further tarnishing the ancient sport.

2010-07-05.title: Pakistan Army Finds Taliban Tough to Root Out
2010-07-05.abstract: Months after declaring victory in South Waziristan and the Swat Valley, the army has been forced to reopen campaigns after militants seeped back in.

2010-07-05.title: Israel Grows Uneasy Over Reliance on Migrant Labor
2010-07-05.abstract: Even as foreign workers have become a mainstay of the Israeli economy, their presence has increasingly clashed with the country’s Zionist ideology.

2010-07-05.title: A Language Thrives in Its Caribbean Home
2010-07-05.abstract: Papiamentu, a Creole language spoken on Curaçao, Bonaire and Aruba, endures as other tongues face extinction.

2010-07-05.title: Once-Dominant Party in Mexico Seems Resurgent
2010-07-05.abstract: The Institutional Revolutionary Party appeared headed for a strong showing in gubernatorial elections Sunday.

2010-07-06.title: Canadian Intelligence Director Repeats Claims of Foreign Influence
2010-07-06.abstract: Richard B. Fadden said told a committee in Parliament that he thinks some Canadian politicians and public servants are under the influence of foreign governments.

2010-07-06.title: Taking a Stand, Then a Deduction
2010-07-06.abstract: Americans, often working through Jewish and Christian charities, are able to receive tax breaks for donations that support settlement activity in the West Bank and East Jerusalem.

2010-07-06.title: Tax-Exempt Funds Aid Settlements in West Bank
2010-07-06.abstract: As the United States seeks to end a four-decade Jewish settlement enterprise, the American Treasury helps sustain the settlements through tax breaks on donations to support them.

2010-07-06.title: An Orphan Family in Haiti
2010-07-06.abstract: In Croix-des-Bouquets, a group of children living together in a makeshift orphanage face an uncertain future.

2010-07-06.title: Haitian Orphans Have Little but One Another
2010-07-06.abstract: A makeshift orphanage and improvised family face an uncertain future as an eviction looms.

2010-07-07.title: A Capitalist Enclave in North Korea Survives
2010-07-07.abstract: At the Kaesong Industrial Complex, 121 mostly South Korean companies to employ 44,000 North Koreans despite rising political tensions.

2010-07-07.title: Vatican Approaches New Abuse Rules
2010-07-07.abstract: Changes being considered would make it easier to discipline pedophile priests and lengthen the statute of limitations.

2010-07-07.title: Canada: Board to Investigate Police Actions at G-20 Meeting
2010-07-07.abstract: The Toronto police were criticized for allowing violence by some protesters to go unchecked one day and overreacting to protests the next day.

2010-07-07.title: Egypt: Bus Driver Kills 6 on Morning Commuting Trip
2010-07-07.abstract: A bus driver for an engineering firm, possibly angered that colleagues cut him out of a scheme to steal antiquities, shot six employees before surrendering to the authorities.

2010-07-07.title: Suspended Sentence for Antiwhaling Activist
2010-07-07.abstract: A Japanese court on Wednesday convicted a New Zealand man of assault and obstructing Japan’s whaling fleet in the Antarctic, but he will not be jailed.

2010-07-08.title: Backers Say Russian Inmate Torn by Possible Deal
2010-07-08.abstract: Family and friends of Igor V. Sutyagin said the former arms control researcher did not want to leave in a spy swap, given his claims of innocence, but he feels he has no choice.

2010-07-08.title: Afghan Companies Say U.S. Did Not Pay Them
2010-07-08.abstract: Afghan construction companies working on contracts for U.S. and NATO  military bases have accused American middlemen of reneging on payments.

2010-07-08.title: Britain: Ruling for Gay Asylum Seekers
2010-07-08.abstract: The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld the right of gay asylum seekers not to be deported if they could show that they faced persecution in their home countries.

2010-07-08.title: Russia: Government Criticizes Clinton
2010-07-08.abstract: The Foreign Ministry criticized Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton for using the word “occupation” in referring to Russian forces in the breakaway Georgian enclaves of Abkhazia and South Ossetia.

2010-07-08.title: China: Dalai Lama’s Birthday Ignored
2010-07-08.abstract: A Foreign Ministry spokesman said China preferred to ignore the Dalai Lama’s birthday and instead remember two dates in modern Tibetan history: those of Tibet’s “peaceful liberation” and Serf Emancipation Day.

2010-07-09.title: The Battle of the Teammates Dominates Talk in Silverstone
2010-07-09.abstract: The championship is coming to its halfway point and the battle of the teammates is increasing on track, as the tension increases off track too.

2010-07-09.title: Smugglers in Iraq Blunt Sanctions Against Iran
2010-07-09.abstract: One of the biggest gaps in the American strategy is on full display in Iraq, where millions of dollars in crude oil are smuggled from Kurdistan every year.

2010-07-09.title: Loophole May Have Aided Theft of Classified Data
2010-07-09.abstract: The soldier accused of downloading data from military computers in Iraq appears to have exploited a loophole in security.

2010-07-09.title: Blunt General Appointed to Lead Forces in Mideast
2010-07-09.abstract: Gen. James N. Mattis would be in charge of American military forces in a strategic area from Egypt through Pakistan, and from Central Asia past the Persian Gulf.

2010-07-09.title: Draft Avoids Condemning North Korea in Ship Attack
2010-07-09.abstract: A Security Council diplomats’ statement on the attack, which killed 46 sailors aboard a South Korean warship, condemns it but bows to China and Russia in not blaming the north.

2010-07-10.title: Chinese Separatists Tied to Norway Bomb Plot
2010-07-10.abstract: Officials said one of the men was a Chinese Uighur, and all three arrested supposedly belonged to a group that advocates separatism in China.

2010-07-10.title: The Adviser at the Heart of National Security
2010-07-10.abstract: Denis McDonough, the National Security Council’s chief of staff, is so close to the president that colleagues will often not make a major move without checking with him.

2010-07-10.title: South Africa Braces for New Attacks on Immigrants
2010-07-10.abstract: Townships and squatter camps are buzzing with threats that once the World Cup is over, a campaign of violence will begin against foreign residents.

2010-07-10.title: Intrigue and Ambiguity in Cases of 4 Russians Sent to West in Spy Swap
2010-07-10.abstract: Some of the cases of the four men show how the definition of spying has grown murkier since relations have warmed between the United States and Russia.

2010-07-10.title: In India, Castes, Honor and Killings Intertwine
2010-07-10.abstract: A woman’s death and the arrest of her mother have drawn attention to a resurgence of so-called honor killings.

2010-07-11.title: Anglican Group Hits Impasse on Women
2010-07-11.abstract: The Church of England’s General Synod rejected a plan to compromise over the ordination of female bishops.

2010-07-11.title: Japan’s Ruling Party Suffers Setback
2010-07-11.abstract: Prime Minister Naoto Kan said that he would not resign, though tallies showed his party heading for a major setback in a vote seen as a referendum on his government.

2010-07-11.title: Webber Wins British Grand Prix, and Settles a Score
2010-07-11.abstract: Mark Webber won the British Grand Prix a day after being treated like a No. 2 driver by his Red Bull team.

2010-07-11.title: Taliban Attacks Kill 11 Afghan Policemen
2010-07-11.abstract: Eleven police officers and a district governor were killed in three separate attacks by the Taliban  in northeast Afghanistan, NATO  and local officials said on Sunday.

2010-07-11.title: Games Give South Africa Hope in Fighting Woes
2010-07-11.abstract: Optimists are talking about the short-term gains and long-term promise the hosting of the tournament allowed.

2010-07-12.title: Cuban Prisoner Releases Shift Focus to Dissidents
2010-07-12.abstract: Cuba’s decision to release 52 political prisoners has done little to quell the island’s fiercest critics, who are asking President Raúl Castro, “What about the rest?”

2010-07-12.title: Bombers Kill More Than 50 in Attacks in Uganda Capital
2010-07-12.abstract: Synchronized bombings struck large gatherings of soccer fans in the normally peaceful capital of Kampala.

2010-07-12.title: Clerics to Work Within Schools of Iran’s Capital
2010-07-12.abstract: A reported plan to send 1,000 clerics to schools in Iran’s capital appears to be part of a wider social and cultural crackdown on the nation’s youth.

2010-07-12.title: Training Soldiers in Pakistan
2010-07-12.abstract: Pakistani Army soldiers undergoing training by Americans at a base southeast of Islamabad.

2010-07-12.title: Distrust Slows U.S. Training of Pakistanis
2010-07-12.abstract: The American-led training program is part of an effort to repair relations with the Pakistani military, government and public.

2010-07-13.title: After Killing at a Bazaar, Iran Declares 2 Days Off
2010-07-13.abstract: The sudden announcement came after a long-simmering dispute between President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad and the Tehran bazaar over taxes erupted last week, leaving one merchant dead.

2010-07-13.title: International Court Adds Genocide to Charges Against Sudan Leader
2010-07-13.abstract: Even genocide charges may not bring President Omar Hassan al-Bashir closer to trial at The Hague any time soon, but they may further complicate his international dealings and travels.

2010-07-13.title: Old Bones Yield a New Age of Dinosaurs in Thailand
2010-07-13.abstract: Giant bones found regularly in the Khorat Plateau of northeastern Thailand turned out to be from dinosaurs.

2010-07-13.title: Abuse Took Years to Ignite Belgian Clergy Inquiry
2010-07-13.abstract: For decades, the nephew of the bishop of Bruges  sought to alert others that he had been molested by his uncle.

2010-07-13.title: After Attacks in Uganda, Worry Grows Over Group
2010-07-13.abstract: Bombings in Uganda deepened worries in the United States about the Shabab, which is spreading terrorism across borders using a playbook written by Al Qaeda.

2010-07-14.title: Argentina Senate to Vote on Gay Marriage
2010-07-14.abstract: Argentina’s Senate is voting Wednesday on a bill granting gays the broadest marital protections in Latin America.

2010-07-14.title: As a Brigade Returns Safe, Some Meet New Enemies
2010-07-14.abstract: After soldiers return to the United States from Iraq, the odds that some of them will die violent deaths continues. The military has shifted some focus to the home front.

2010-07-14.title: Iran: Strike by Merchants Spreads at Tehran Bazaar
2010-07-14.abstract: The strike, the second major protest since 1979 at the bazaar, began after rumors circulated that the government planned to increase taxes up to 70 percent.

2010-07-14.title: Trying to Stop Cattle Burps From Heating Up Planet
2010-07-14.abstract: A team of microbiologists and genetic researchers in Australia are looking for ways to reduce livestock carbon emissions.

2010-07-14.title: Focus on the Home Front
2010-07-14.abstract: The soldiers of the Fourth Brigade, First Armored Division have been home from Iraq for three months now, but the odds that some of them will die violent deaths continues.

2010-07-15.title: Pius Njawé, Noted African Journalist, Dies at 53
2010-07-15.abstract: Mr. Njawé was considered a symbol of opposition to the autocratic regime of Paul Biya in Cameroon, who has been in power since 1982 in the resource-rich but poor Central African nation.

2010-07-15.title: Marrack Goulding, Former Chief Peacekeeper for the U.N., Dies at 73
2010-07-15.abstract: Mr. Goulding, a former British ambassador to Angola, led United Nations peacekeeping operations from 1986 to 1993, at times coming under fire himself.

2010-07-15.title: Records Show Doubts on ’64 Vietnam Crisis
2010-07-15.abstract: Previously classified transcripts show senators of the time questioning whether the White House and the Pentagon had deceived them over the 1964 Gulf of Tonkin incident.

2010-07-15.title: South Korea: Military Exercises Planned With the United States
2010-07-15.abstract: The United States and South Korea are expected to hold joint military exercises soon in the Yellow Sea, a response to the sinking of a South Korean warship in March, the Pentagon said Wednesday.

2010-07-15.title: Video Shows Failed Bomber Before Attack
2010-07-15.abstract: An Arabic-language television channel has broadcast excerpts of what it said was a martyrdom video by Faisal Shahzad, who pleaded guilty last month to trying to detonate a bomb  in Times Square.

2010-07-16.title: China: Charges in Wrongful Jailing Case
2010-07-16.abstract: Five officers in Henan Province have been charged with forcing a confession from Zhao Zuohai, who was wrongly imprisoned, according to China Daily.

2010-07-16.title: Sponsor of Flotilla Tied to Elite of Turkey
2010-07-16.abstract: The charity behind the Gaza flotilla, which ended in an Israeli raid, had support from Turkey’s governing party.

2010-07-16.title: Netherlands: Friction in the Hague
2010-07-16.abstract: After a clash between judges and prosecutors over evidence, judges at the International Criminal Court ordered a Congolese warlord conditionally freed.

2010-07-16.title: Tanzania: Tribunal Lawyer Killed
2010-07-16.abstract: Jwani Mwaikusa, a lawyer for the United Nations tribunal for the Rwandan genocide was shot in front of his home in Dar es Salaam.

2010-07-16.title: Israel: Military Court Convicts 2
2010-07-16.abstract: An Israeli Army officer and a soldier were found guilty of unbecoming conduct for their involvement in shooting a rubber bullet at the foot of a bound and blindfolded Palestinian in 2008.

2010-07-17.title: Pakistani Legislators Face Accusations of Faking Their Degrees
2010-07-17.abstract: In a country where politicians have long dodged accountability, the issue of fake degrees has provoked outrage — as well as laughter — among the public.

2010-07-17.title: A Star Keeps Rocking in the Not-So-Free World
2010-07-17.abstract: Yuri Shevchuk, a musician, remains a voice of defiance in Russia, taking Vladimir V. Putin to task in a recent meeting.

2010-07-17.title: Step Taken to End Impasse Over Cybersecurity Talks
2010-07-17.abstract: A United Nations-backed panel’s report seeks to resolve differences between the United States and Russia over how to deal with threats to the Internet.

2010-07-17.title: Mexico: 4 Die in Car Bombing, Thought to Be First in Drug War
2010-07-17.abstract: The secretary of public security said that the attack appeared to be in retaliation for the arrest earlier in the day of a top drug lieutenant fpr the Juárez cartel.

2010-07-17.title: Kyrgyzstan: European Group to Send Police to Monitor for Ethnic Violence
2010-07-17.abstract: The Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe agreed to a request by Kyrgyzstan’s provisional government on Friday to send police monitors to the south of Kyrgyzstan.

2010-07-18.title: W.T.O. Ruling Upsets Chief of Jet Maker
2010-07-18.abstract: “We are in an abnormal and increasingly unfair situation,” Louis Gallois, the chief of EADS, said of the bidding for a big Air Force contract.

2010-07-18.title: Dozens Killed in Iraq Suicide Attacks
2010-07-18.abstract: More than 40 people were killed when a man detonated himself outside an Iraqi Army base as Awakening members lined up to receive paychecks.

2010-07-18.title: Prison Break Precedes Afghan Conference
2010-07-18.abstract: Insurgents used a prison break, a suicide attack and homemade bombs to make their presence felt just two days before a major international conference.

2010-07-18.title: Wasps to Fight Thai Cassava Plague
2010-07-18.abstract: The first wave of an army of 250,000 wasps was deployed to eradicate a plague of mealybugs that threatens to devastate Thailand’s $1.5 billion crop of cassava plants.

2010-07-18.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan Sign a Trade Deal, Representing a Thaw in Relations
2010-07-18.abstract: Afghanistan and Pakistan signed a landmark trade agreement, symbolizing a recent thaw between the two long-suspicious neighbors.

2010-07-19.title: Tax-Free Living in Pakistan
2010-07-19.abstract: So few of the Pakistani rich pay taxes that one neighborhood hired transgender tax collectors to embarrass residents into paying. Adam B. Ellick follows them a week before the tax deadline.

2010-07-19.title: Pakistan’s Elite Pay Few Taxes, Widening Gap
2010-07-19.abstract: The lack of a workable tax system feeds an inequality in Pakistani society, and is helping an insurgency spread.

2010-07-19.title: Pakistan Hires Transgender Workers to Shame Tax Delinquents
2010-07-19.abstract: The board of a posh Pakistan neighborhood has hired a team of transgendered tax collectors to go door to door to embarrass the rich until they pay.

2010-07-19.title: Virginia Man Flies Home on Waiver
2010-07-19.abstract: Yahya Wehelie, who was prevented for weeks from returning to the United States after studying in Yemen, remains on the no-fly list.

2010-07-19.title: Above the Crowds, but Not Above Lawbreaking
2010-07-19.abstract: Roofers, a youth subculture taking shape in Moscow, delight in gaining access to buildings just for a chance to look at the surrounding landscape from the roofs.

2010-07-20.title: Afghan Plan on Transfer of Security Gets Support
2010-07-20.abstract: Western nations at a conference on Tuesday are expected to endorse President Hamid Karzai’s plan for Afghan forces to take the lead by 2014.

2010-07-20.title: Iraq’s Conflict, Reflected in a Family Tragedy
2010-07-20.abstract: It reads like a short version of the war: a man who worked for the Americans was killed by his son, an insurgent.

2010-07-20.title: Petraeus’s Successor Is Known for Impolitic Words
2010-07-20.abstract: Gen. James N. Mattis is admired on the front lines but has been occasionally seen by his civilian superiors as too rough-edged.

2010-07-20.title: Europe Considers New Penalties for Iran
2010-07-20.abstract: The measures are meant to put additional pressure on Iranian leaders to curb the nation’s nuclear program.

2010-07-20.title: Abuse Allegations Reported in Peacekeeping Missions
2010-07-20.abstract: There have been 45 allegations of sexual exploitation and abuse against personnel in United Nations peacekeeping  and special political missions worldwide this year.

2010-07-21.title: Water Dispute Increases India-Pakistan Tension
2010-07-21.abstract: A battle over a dam threatens to upset delicate negotiations to renew peace talks on Kashmir.

2010-07-21.title: Ex-Official Says Afghan and Iraq Wars Increased Threats to Britain
2010-07-21.abstract: The former leader of MI5, Britain’s domestic intelligence agency, said that evidence did not justify invading Iraq and that the war radicalized young Muslims.

2010-07-21.title: Turkey: Kurdish Separatists Kill 6 Soldiers, Military Says
2010-07-21.abstract: Militants from the Kurdistan Workers Party, or P.K.K., fired on an army unit Monday night, setting off a firefight that lasted until morning.

2010-07-21.title: Report Says U.S. Fails to Assess Drug Aid to Mexico
2010-07-21.abstract: A report found that the State Department had not set specific targets to determine whether antidrug money was disrupting crime and reforming law enforcement.

2010-07-21.title: Britain’s Leader Carves Identity as Budget Cutter
2010-07-21.abstract: After 10 weeks in office, David Cameron has emerged as one of the most activist prime ministers in modern times.

2010-07-22.title: Afghan Deadline Is Cutting Two Ways
2010-07-22.abstract: The timetable to draw down troops hangs over President Obama as much as it does over the Afghan government.

2010-07-22.title: Conflict Resurfaces With Lessons on Nation-Building
2010-07-22.abstract: The continuing tension over Kosovo serves as a reminder of just how challenging the enterprise of nation-building really is.

2010-07-22.title: Somali Refugees Fear Loss of Ugandan Haven
2010-07-22.abstract: Increasing violent attacks and the departure of a resettlement agency have threatened a paradise for Somali refugees in Uganda.

2010-07-22.title: Peter Walls, General in Zimbabwe, Dies at 83
2010-07-22.abstract: Lt. Gen. Walls played a central and sometimes ambiguous role in the first days of Zimbabwe’s transition to majority rule.

2010-07-22.title: Gaza: 2 Militants Killed by Israeli Shells
2010-07-22.abstract: Two Palestinian  militants were killed and six were wounded Wednesday in an Israeli shelling in the northern Gaza Strip, Palestinian witnesses and officials at a hospital said.

2010-07-23.title: Europe Turns Ear Toward Voice of the People
2010-07-23.abstract: The European Union is about to give its 500 million citizens more say in proposed legislation through petitions. Some see trouble brewing.

2010-07-23.title: White House Presses Republicans on Arms Treaty
2010-07-23.abstract: The Obama administration pushes for the ratification of a new arms control treaty with Russia before fall election campaigns intensify partisan divisions.

2010-07-23.title: Russia: Post-Swap Change of Heart
2010-07-23.abstract: A Russian released from a lengthy prison term by Moscow as part of a spy exchange with the United States said Thursday that he wanted to return to Russia.

2010-07-23.title: Lebanon: Hezbollah Chief Expects Indictments in Ex-premier’s Death
2010-07-23.abstract: Hassan Nasrallah, the leader of the Lebanese Shiite movement Hezbollah, said Thursday that he had been told that members of the group would be indicted by a United Nations tribunal investigating the 2005 killing of former Prime Minister Rafik Hariri of Lebanon.

2010-07-23.title: China: Floods Kill Hundreds
2010-07-23.abstract: At least 273 people have died and 218 have been reported missing from recent flooding along the Yangtze River and other bodies of water, according to Xinhua, the state news agency.

2010-07-24.title: McChrystal Ends Service With Regret and a Laugh
2010-07-24.abstract: The ceremony included pageantry and the general’s admission that he was not leaving on his own terms.

2010-07-24.title: Guineans Will Bolster Peace Efforts in Somalia
2010-07-24.abstract: The decision to send hundreds of troops to Somalia came after Somali insurgents claimed responsibility for an attack in Uganda during the World Cup.

2010-07-24.title: Turkey: 102 Military Officers Charged With Plotting Coup
2010-07-24.abstract: An Istanbul court on Friday ordered the arrest of 102 active and retired military officers, charging them with plotting to overthrow Prime Minister Tayyip Erdogan’s government in 2003.

2010-07-24.title: Netherlands: Oil Trading Company Fined in Dumping of Toxic Sludge
2010-07-24.abstract: A Dutch court on Friday imposed the maximum fine of $1.28 million dollars on the oil trading company Trafigura for illegally exporting highly toxic sludge.

2010-07-24.title: Military Send-Off for McChrystal
2010-07-24.abstract: Gen. Stanley A. McChrystal, who was fired by President Obama last month after disparaging remarks by him and his staff members about top civilian leaders appeared in Rolling Stone magazine, retired in a military ceremony Friday night at Fort McNair.

2010-07-25.title: Stampede at German Music Festival
2010-07-25.abstract: Panic as a crowd surged through a jammed entrance tunnel killed at least 18 people at the Love Parade festival in Duisburg in western Germany.

2010-07-25.title: Vettel on Pole, by the Thinnest of Margins
2010-07-25.abstract: Sebastian Vettel beat Fernando Alonso to the pole at the German Grand Prix by two thousandths of a second, while Michael Schumacher finished a hundredth behind his teammate.

2010-07-25.title: As Tensions Rise, U.S. and S. Korea Begin Drills
2010-07-25.abstract: North Korean officials threatened to respond to the show of force, which involved one of the largest American nuclear-powered aircraft carriers.

2010-07-25.title: Search Widens for U.S. Sailors in Afghanistan
2010-07-25.abstract: As a manhunt by Afghan and American forces widened on Sunday, a Taliban spokesman said that one of two American sailors ambushed there was killed.

2010-07-25.title: Teammate Lets Alonso Pass Him to Win German Grand Prix
2010-07-25.abstract: Fernando Alonso won the German Grand Prix for Ferrari on Sunday with his teammate, Felipe Massa, finishing second. But the focus of the attention after the race was on how Massa had allowed Alonso to pass him for the win.

2010-07-26.title: Japanese Village Creates Art From Hues of Rice
2010-07-26.abstract: Every year, villagers create large and complex images using rice paddies as canvas and plants as paint.

2010-07-26.title: Hearing on Lockerbie Sets Off More Finger-Pointing
2010-07-26.abstract: The coming Senate hearing in Washington has prompted more disavowals by British and Scottish officials involved in releasing a convicted Libyan bomber.

2010-07-26.title: As English Spreads, Indonesians Fear for Their Language
2010-07-26.abstract: Indonesia’s linguistic legacy is under threat as growing numbers of wealthy and upper-middle-class families turn to private schools that focus on English and devote little time to Bahasa Indonesia.

2010-07-26.title: Brazil’s President Works to Lend Popularity to a Protégée
2010-07-26.abstract: As President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva pushes to help his chief of staff get elected, whether he can transfer his immense popularity is unclear.

2010-07-26.title: Mexican Officials Say Prisoners Acted as Hit Men
2010-07-26.abstract: Inmates were allowed out at night to carry out murder-for-hire jobs using jail guards’ weapons and vehicles, officials said Sunday.

2010-07-27.title: In Cuba, Revolution Day Without Castro at Podium
2010-07-27.abstract: Raúl Castro’s choice not to speak at one of the most important fixtures on the Cuban calendar disappointed and puzzled many.

2010-07-27.title: Honduras Faces Criticism Over Journalist Killings After a Coup
2010-07-27.abstract: A report by the Committee to Protect Journalists says the government’s failure to investigate the killings of seven journalists has fostered “a climate of lawlessness.”

2010-07-27.title: Yemen: Fighting Intensifies Between Rebels and Government
2010-07-27.abstract: Dozens of people were killed in battles between Houthi rebels and tribesmen loyal to the government, according to witnesses and government officials.

2010-07-27.title: $700,000 Awarded to Official Passed Over for Promotion
2010-07-27.abstract: A former senior official in the Department of Economic and Social Affairs was awarded the money for breach of contract, a decision the world organization will appeal.

2010-07-27.title: Iraqi Militants Stealing Blood for the Injured
2010-07-27.abstract: Guards at medical facilities have often stood by as the robberies take place, according to workers.

2010-07-28.title: Diplomat Harsh on Leaders in Testimony for Iraq Inquiry
2010-07-28.abstract: Hans Blix, who led a weapons inspection team in the prelude to the Iraq invasion, testified as part of the British inquiry into the war.

2010-07-28.title: Russia's YouTube Whistle-Blower
2010-07-28.abstract: A police officer in Russia uses the Internet to challenge the country's leaders to address rampant corruption.

2010-07-28.title: Democrats Split as House Backs War Funds
2010-07-28.abstract: The House approved a war funding bill, but Democrats splintered after a disclosure of battlefield documents.

2010-07-28.title: Cameron Urges Change in Gaza
2010-07-28.abstract: The British prime minister condemned Israel’s partial blockade of Gaza, describing Gaza as a “prison camp,” and urged Turkey and Israel to repair their friendship.

2010-07-28.title: More Troops for Somalia, but No Peace to Keep
2010-07-28.abstract: Analysts say sending more peacekeepers to Somalia is the wrong approach because outside forces have been unable to stabilize the country thus far.

2010-07-29.title: In China, Pollution Worsens Despite New Efforts
2010-07-29.abstract: China continues to suffer the downsides of its rampant economic growth, with new figures showing an increase in pollution in the air and along its waterways.

2010-07-29.title: U.N. Appoints New Director of Troubled Watchdog Group
2010-07-29.abstract: After years of battles over the agency’s independence, the General Assembly appointed a new director for the Office of Internal Oversight Services.

2010-07-29.title: Iran: Plants to Use Benzene to Increase Gas Production
2010-07-29.abstract: Trying to offset the effect of sanctions, Iran said Wednesday that it would increase gasoline production by converting two petrochemical plants so they could produce gasoline.

2010-07-29.title: Informants Who Aided U.S. Named in Classified Reports
2010-07-29.abstract: Some of the tens of thousands of Pentagon documents released this week by WikiLeaks contain identifying information about Afghan informants who aided United States and NATO forces, potentially jeopardizing their future cooperation or even putting them at risk of reprisal.

2010-07-29.title: U.S. Military Scrutinizes Leaks for Risks to Afghans
2010-07-29.abstract: Some of the documents released by WikiLeaks contain the names of Afghans who gave information to NATO troops.

2010-07-30.title: Gates Assails WikiLeaks Over Release of Reports
2010-07-30.abstract: The defense secretary said the breach had endangered lives and damaged the ability of others to trust the U.S.

2010-07-30.title: Taliban Make Inroads in Strategic Province
2010-07-30.abstract: In the strategic northern province of Baghlan, Afghans deprived of jobs and government services  are turning to Taliban courts for speedy justice and drifting toward those who will pay them — either local strongmen or the Taliban.

2010-07-30.title: Taliban Exploit Openings in Neglected Province
2010-07-30.abstract: Deprived of jobs and government services, people in Baghlan Province are turning to the Taliban for speedy justice and work.

2010-07-30.title: The Stone-Throwing Kids
2010-07-30.abstract: A quarter-century conflict between the Turkey and the Kurdistan Workers Party has led, in recent years, to the arrests of Kurdish youths, sometimes merely for attending a demonstration, chanting a slogan or throwing a stone.

2010-07-30.title: Turkey Softens Law That Jailed Young Kurds
2010-07-30.abstract: Lawmakers reduced the sentences of hundreds of Kurdish youths, some of them guilty only of attending protests.

2010-07-31.title: Brazilian Goalie Is Charged in Ex-Lover’s Killing
2010-07-31.abstract: Bruno Fernandes das Dores de Souza is accused of murder in a gruesome case that has fascinated much of Brazil.

2010-07-31.title: Sudan: Violent Clashes Erupt Over Peace Talks for Darfur
2010-07-31.abstract: The fighting, which pitted supporters of peace talks  against opponents of reconciliation, left about 10 people dead.

2010-07-31.title: Russia: Chairwoman Steps Down From Human Rights Council
2010-07-31.abstract: Ella Pamfilova apparently resigned after growing disenchanted with the political situation in Russia.

2010-07-31.title: Families of Hikers Jailed in Iran Mark First Anniversary With Rally
2010-07-31.abstract: A protest rally on Friday outside the Iranian Mission to the United Nations was the first of around 30 similar protests organized by friends of the three in cities around the world.

2010-07-31.title: Army Broadens Inquiry Into WikiLeaks Disclosure
2010-07-31.abstract: Investigators were focusing on a group of Pfc. Bradley Manning’s friends and acquaintances in Massachusetts.
